Instead, the decision was quietly posted on the committee's website." | "The company expressed frustration over the lack of transparency and clarity regarding which entity was granted the exploration rights." || Governance | Reputation & Social License | Mining image and public perception -> "Meanwhile shares of Midnight Sun Mining Corp. (TSXV: MMA) fell sharply on Thursday after the company announced that the Zambian government had rejected its request to renew the exploration license for the Kazhiba target at its flagship Solwezi copper project." | "The news sent Midnight Sun's shares plummeting 11% on the Toronto Stock Exchange, closing at C$0.355 per share, bringing the company's market capitalization to C$58.7 million ($43.5 million)."

title: Zambia plans State firm to own 30% of critical minerals mines
impact_evidence: Governance | Regulation, Compliance & Institutions | Legislative and regulatory framework effectiveness -> "It will also review Zambia's policy and regulatory environment to restrict the export of unprocessed materials." | "The state will set up a special purpose vehicle to invest in critical minerals under a design framework that includes a "production sharing mechanism" setting aside a minimum 30% of the output from new mining projects, according to the document unveiled by Kabuswe in Zambia's capital, Lusaka." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Business opportunities (local supply chains, services) -> "The government also intends to make investors in the critical minerals sector allocate at least 35% of procurement costs to local suppliers, according to the strategy." || Social | Equity, Rights & Vulnerable Groups | Wealth distribution and inequality -> "Zambia plans to establish an investment company that will control at least 30% of critical minerals production from future mines." | "The state will set up a special purpose vehicle to invest in critical minerals under a design framework that includes a "production sharing mechanism" setting aside a minimum 30% of the output from new mining projects, according to the document unveiled by Kabuswe in Zambia's capital, Lusaka."

impact_evidence: Environmental | Land, Soil & Geology | Transport corridors and access road impacts -> "offering a cargo transport route from Zambia's copper and cobalt mines to the sea on Tanzania's coast that bypasses South Africa and the former state of Rhodesia." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Business opportunities (local supply chains, services) -> "the World Bank approved $270 million in financing to help improve connectivity between neighbors Tanzania and Zambia and boost regional trade." || Social | Livelihoods & Economy | Employment creation and job security -> "The multi-year project had involved the construction of two dozen tunnels and hundreds of bridges by tens of thousands of Chinese and African workers."
